title: The Big Cheese Live Multi-Catch Mouse Trap, Humane, Baited and Ready-To-Use, Catches 4 Mice to Release, Self-Setting, Plastic, Indoor and Outdoor Use (Pack of 2)
features: The information below applies to each unit in the pack
ALL-IN-ONE KIT: Pre-baited with Fresh ‘n Tasty bait attractant which is proven to catch more mice, ready-to-use
EASY-TO-USE: Self-setting mechanism with clear view sliding lid for easy inspection and release
MULTI-CATCH: Humane, welfare-friendly, reusable live catch mouse trap catches 4 or more mice alive in one setting without causing injury
REUSABLE: Captured mice can be released without the need for handling
EFFECTIVE: Mechanism ensures that the captured mouse cannot escape
SAFE: A suitable alternative to poisons and snap traps for situations where children and pets may be present
RE-BAIT: Use The Big Cheese Mouse and Rat Attractant (sold separately)
description: From the manufacturer OVER 25 YEARS OF RODENT CONTROL MADE EASY! Catch & Kill: We offer a wide selection of conventional spring traps with a powerful snap and hold. Our Fresh 'n Tasty range is ready-baited with The Big Cheese Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse & Rat Attractant - a poison-free formula proven to dramatically increase catch rate. Pet-safe and easy-set options are also available making The Big Cheese Catch & Kill range suitable for use in most areas of the household. Bait & Kill: Our Home Choice range offers a bait for every situation, and with 50% LESS POISON than previous formulations, it is the ideal choice for use in and around the home. The range includes damp proof wax blocks suitable for use in all weather conditions, chew thru grain bait sachets for easy bait placement, and highly palatable fresh pasta bait recommended for food preparation areas. Follow our easy on-pack icons to choose the most effective bait for your situation. Catch Alive: Safe for use around children and pets, these traps provide a humane, welfare-friendly alternative to lethal methods. Our professional-quality cage traps and ready-baited mouse traps have been designed with safety in mind, both for the user and the target pest. Easy-set mechanisms and fast-action closures allow for efficient rodent trapping. Repel: 24/7 protection and peace of mind - our advanced ultrasonic repellers will protect your home around the clock, ridding your home of rodent pests and preventing their return. Plus a new selection of low-cost consumables designed to help defend homes from rodent infestations. Poison-free and safe for use around children. Ready-to-use, ready-baited mouse trap Rodent control made easy! When mice and rats move in, it's time to bring in The Big Cheese! Choose from a comprehensive range of tried and tested traps, deterrents and baits within 4 straight-forward methods of control. Live Multi-Catch Mouse Trap from The Big Cheese Fresh baited, welfare-friendly mouse trap. The Live Multi-Catch Mouse Trap is an effective and welfare-friendly way to control mice in and around the home. In severely affected areas use more than one trap: mice live in groups and traps placed 2-3 metres apart will ensure the maximum catch. Each trap catches 4 or more mice alive. Baited, ready-to-use trap catches 4 or more mice alive in one setting. Self-setting mechanism with clear view sliding lid for easy inspection and release of catch. Poison-free – safe to use around children and pets. Refill and refresh attractant with STV163 The Big Cheese Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse & Rat Attractant (sold separately). Part of the comprehensive range of rodent control products from The Big Cheese. Poison free Fresh 'n Tasty bait Poison free and proven to attract rodents with an incredible catch rate compared to scented pedals and other traditional attractants. Live catch When catching live animals it is your responsibility to check a set trap every two hours, removing traps when regular inspection is not possible. For live release, hold trap just above the ground with door facing away from you and open. Reusable Replenish traps with Fresh 'n Tasty Mouse and Rat Attractant (purchased separately) and re-set. Mice Mice are a highly adaptive and invasive species and they’re able to populate a variety of ecosystems. Agricultural fields, buildings, and boats are all viable habitats. In more temperate climates there will be even more possible nest sites. Swimming, climbing, jumping proficiency, and the two incisors on their upper jaw, all make for a formidable predator wherever they reside. However, despite all of these amazing abilities, the house mouse chooses to reside, as its name would indicate, in a home. House mice ( Mus Musculus Domesticus ) are a highly adaptive member of the genus Mus (mouse), yet for some reason they choose to live in the least demanding environment, a human home. They are attracted by the continuous flow of food and warmth, ideal for nest sites. The offer of food and shelter is so tempting that many choose to stay and breed year-round. Diet As one can imagine, the palate of a house mouse is highly adaptable since every habitat they migrate through will hold different opportunities for feeding. Outside dwellers will eat insects and a lot of grain, however, inside dwelling mice are practically omnivorous and, just like brown rats, will consume 10-15% of their body weight each day in just about anything we throw away. Most house mice will nibble on multiple different food sources to obtain their required nutrition, up to 20 times each night, since they’re nocturnal. A large proportion of a mouse’s 3g daily intake will be grain, namely cereal, favouring high-energy, man-made foods. Studies have proven that the levels of aggression in both rats and mice can be attributed to this high fat ‘human’ diet they have both been on for so long. These foods increase oestrogen levels and results in a shorter temper and longer periods of aggression. Evidence of intrusion 3-6 mm long droppings, black and rod-shaped. Approximately 80 per day, often appearing in clusters. Spotted in various places including attics, storage boxes, floorboards, disused cupboards. Check around fridges, ovens, heating units and washing machines too. Check wood, paper, cables, carpet and furniture for marks. Dark grey marks on surfaces from oil on hair. Although they don’t sweat, they emit a musky odour and urinate a lot, producing an ammonia smell. Hearing pattering feet in walls at night could be a sign of a mouse or rat infestation. If your dog is barking at night at what seems to be nothing, it could be picking up the scent or sounds of rodents.
